sections.title: Some and any
sections.content:
**Some** – when we have something (positive):
• I have **some** milk. There are **some** books.

**Any** – in questions and when we don't have (negative):
• Do you have **any** milk? I don't have **any** money.

sections.title: Much and many
sections.content:
**Much** – with uncountable:
• How **much** water? I don't have **much** time.

**Many** – with countable:
• How **many** books? There aren't **many** people.

sections.title: A lot of
sections.content:
**A lot of** – both countable and uncountable; when we have a lot:
• I have **a lot of** friends. She has **a lot of** work.

questions.question: How _____ water do you need?
questions.explanation: Water is uncountable. We use much.

questions.question: How _____ books do you have?
questions.explanation: Books are countable. We use many.
